message:
feat(tantivy_reindex): Cx-first reindex_range_observed — trail complete (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Final slice on the tantivy_reindex Cx-first trail. Adds the
observed-variant entry point so all 4 public async methods on
ReindexPipeline now have `_with_cx` siblings.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `ReindexPipeline::reindex_range_observed_with_cx(cx, storage,
    source, from, to, consumer_id, batch_size, dedup, schema,
    observer)` — Cx-first parallel of `reindex_range_observed`.
    Pre-flight checkpoint gates the empty-range short-circuit
    AND preserves `observer.on_complete(&zero_stats)` for the
    trivial empty-range case (semantic: an uncancelled empty call
    still emits a completion signal to the observer).

Observer cancellation semantic (new contract, documented):
  * An uncancelled call emits `on_progress` after each committed
    batch and `on_complete` exactly once at the end — identical to
    legacy behavior.
  * A mid-run cancelled call propagates as
    `Err(IndexerError::Config(...))` WITHOUT invoking
    `on_complete`. The observer only hears completion when the
    run actually completed. This matches the non-cx error path
    (io / write errors also return `Err` without `on_complete`)
    so existing observers do not need to special-case
    cancellation — they can treat `Err` return uniformly.

The loop body is a near-copy of the legacy version with
`cx.checkpoint()?` at the top of each iteration. Factoring the
observer callbacks + cursor loop into a shared helper would
require threading the observer, `start_ms`, `last_progress_ms`,
and `total_estimate` state through `index_loop_exclusive_with_cx`
— deferred to avoid bloating the helper's signature.

Regression test (new)
`reindex_range_observed_with_cx_matches_legacy`:

  * Two separate AppendLogRecorderStorage directories with
    identical 10-event populations.
  * Both variants run `[0, 10)` range with batch_size=5 (forces
    multiple batches + multiple observer.on_progress invocations).
  * Asserts FOUR-axis parity:
    1. ReindexProgress across all 7 counter fields.
    2. ReindexStats across event_count, indexed_count,
       skipped_count, filtered_count, caught_up (duration_ms
       excluded — wall-clock dependent).
    3. Observer callback parity: same on_progress invocation
       count, on_complete fires exactly once on each side.
    4. Indexed doc event_id slice bit-for-bit equivalent.

tantivy_reindex.rs Cx-first trail COMPLETE. All 4 ReindexPipeline
public async entry points now have Cx-first siblings:
  * `full_reindex`            ✓ (tick 55)
  * `reindex_range`           ✓ (tick 56)
  * `backfill`                ✓ (tick 57)
  * `reindex_range_observed`  ✓ (tick 58, this commit)

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * tantivy_reindex:: — 94 passed / 0 failed (tick 57: 93 -> tick
    58: 94, +1 new parity test)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (32.29s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
